title: QUIC RFC 9000 Migration Path Probing and Recovery Reset Verification
verification_method: Execution and inspection of the migration-aware path-probing runtime plus the requirement-home proof for the RFC 9000 Section 9 migration slice.
scope:
This verification covers RFC 9000 Sections 9.1 and 9.2 for the runtime-owned migration slice.
It checks that new local addresses remain candidate paths until path validation succeeds, that the runtime emits a PATH_CHALLENGE before treating the path as normal send traffic, that migration promotion resets congestion, RTT, and ECN validation state, and that port-only address changes can retain the prior recovery snapshot.
It does not claim a generalized multipath scheduler or broader recovery policy outside the migration promotion path.
expected_result: A new local address is probed before normal use, path validation remains explicit, migration resets congestion/RTT/ECN validation state unless only the peer port changed, ECN revalidation restarts on the new path, and the requirement-home proof stays limited to the migration slice.
procedure: Run the requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P1-0001, REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P1-0002, REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P2-0001, REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P2-0002, REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P2-0003, REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P2-0004, and REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S9P2-0005.
Inspect the runtime path records and recovery snapshots for the candidate, validated, and active path transitions.
Confirm that promotion emits the reset path for non-port-only migration and preserves the recovery snapshot only for port-only changes.
preconditions: The RFC 9000 Section 9 requirement-home tests are discoverable by the test runner.
status_summary: This verification artifact records the landed Section 9 migration slice, including path validation before new-local-address use, migration promotion reset behavior, port-only retention, and ECN revalidation, and points at the requirement-home and runtime evidence in the working tree.
